SubItems.Text: Trip Stop by Trip Id GET
SubItems.Label: http-get

SubItems.Text: OAuth Scope Furkot Auth Access Code

showFullCode.title: Show Complete File
showFullCode.description: Show complete file for code, with bootstrap and error handling included.

arrival.description: arrival at the stop in its local timezone as YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm

departure.description: departure from the stop in its local timezone as YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm

passthru.description: true for pass-through points anchoring route

url.description: url of the page with more information about the stop

begin.description: begin of the trip in its local timezone as YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm

description.description: description of the trip (truncated to 200 characters)

end.description: end of the trip in its local timezone as YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm

polyline.description: route path compatible with Google polyline encoding algorithm

expires_in.description: Time in seconds before the access token expires

scope.description:
List of scopes granted
This is a space-delimited list of strings.

expiry.description: Time of token expiry as unix timestamp (UTC)

refresh_token.description:
Refresh token
Used to get a new access token when it expires.

error_description.description:
Gets or sets human-readable text providing additional information on error.
Used to assist the client developer in understanding the error that occurred.

error_uri.description: Gets or sets a URI identifying a human-readable web page with information about the error, used to provide the client developer with additional information about the error.

x-enum-elements.name: invalid_request
x-enum-elements.description: The request is missing a required parameter, includes an unsupported parameter value (other than grant type), repeats a parameter, includes multiple credentials, utilizes more than one mechanism for authenticating the client, or is otherwise malformed.

x-enum-elements.name: invalid_client
x-enum-elements.description: Client authentication failed (e.g., unknown client, no client authentication included, or unsupported authentication method).

x-enum-elements.name: invalid_grant
x-enum-elements.description: The provided authorization grant (e.g., authorization code, resource owner credentials) or refresh token is invalid, expired, revoked, does not match the redirection URI used in the authorization request, or was issued to another client.

x-enum-elements.name: unauthorized_client
x-enum-elements.description: The authenticated client is not authorized to use this authorization grant type.

x-enum-elements.name: unsupported_grant_type
x-enum-elements.description: The authorization grant type is not supported by the authorization server.

x-enum-elements.name: invalid_scope
x-enum-elements.description: The requested scope is invalid, unknown, malformed, or exceeds the scope granted by the resource owner.

Nodes.Text:
Furkot provides Rest API to access user trip data.
Using Furkot API an application can list user trips and display stops for a specific trip.
Furkot API uses OAuth2 protocol to authorize applications to access data on behalf of users.
Furkot API description: [https://help.furkot.com/widgets/furkot-api.html](https://help.furkot.com/widgets/furkot-api.html)
The SDK requires **Go version 1.18 or above**.
Resolve all the SDK dependencies, using the `go get` command.
The following section explains how to use the furkotTrips library in a new project.
- Add the following lines to your application's `go.mod` file:

```go
replace furkotTrips => ".\\Random Directory" // local path to the SDK

require furkotTrips v0.0.0
```
- Resolve the dependencies in the updated `go.mod` file, using the `go get` command.

Steps.Title: Add SDK as a Dependency to the Application

Nodes.Text: The SDK can be configured to use a different environment for making API calls. Available environments are:
Fields
The following parameters are configurable for the API Client:

Data: Configurable http client options like timeout and retries.
Represents the logger configurations for API calls
The Credentials Setter for OAuth 2 Authorization Code Grant
The Credentials Setter for OAuth 2 Implicit Grant

Nodes.Text: The API client can be initialized as follows:

This API uses the following authentication schemes.
* [`furkot_auth_access_code (OAuth 2 Authorization Code Grant)`]($h/__auth_FurkotAuthAccessCode)
* [`furkot_auth_implicit (OAuth 2 Implicit Grant)`]($h/__auth_FurkotAuthImplicit)
Documentation for accessing and setting credentials for furkot_auth_access_code.

Data: OAuth 2 Redirection endpoint or Callback Uri
Object for storing information about the OAuth token
List of scopes that apply to the OAuth token

Nodes.Text: **Note:** Required auth credentials can be set using `WithFurkotAuthAccessCodeCredentials()` by providing a credentials instance with `NewFurkotAuthAccessCodeCredentials()` in the configuration initialization and accessed using the `FurkotAuthAccessCodeCredentials()` method in the configuration instance.
You must initialize the client with *OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Grant* credentials as shown in the following code snippet.

Your application must obtain user authorization before it can execute an endpoint call in case this SDK chooses to use *OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Grant*. This authorization includes the following steps

To obtain user's consent, you must redirect the user to the authorization page.The `BuildAuthorizationURL` method creates the URL to the authorization page. You must have initialized the client with scopes for which you need permission to access.
Once the user responds to the consent request, the OAuth 2.0 server responds to your application's access request by redirecting the user to the redirect URI specified set in `Configuration`.
If the user approves the request, the authorization code will be sent as the `code` query string:
https://example.com/oauth/callback?code=XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX
If the user does not approve the request, the response contains an `error` query string:
https://example.com/oauth/callback?error=access_denied
After the server receives the code, it can exchange this for an *access token*. The access token is an object containing information for authorizing client requests and refreshing the token itself.

Steps.Title: 4\. Authorize the client using the code

Nodes.Text: Scopes enable your application to only request access to the resources it needs while enabling users to control the amount of access they grant to your application. Available scopes are defined in the [`OauthScopeFurkotAuthAccessCode`]($m/OAuth%20ScopeFurkotAuthAccessCode) enumeration.
An access token may expire after sometime. To extend its lifetime, you must refresh the token.
If a token expires, an error will be returned before the next endpoint call requiring authentication.
It is recommended that you store the access token for reuse.

oauthToken := client.Configuration().FurkotAuthAccessCodeCredentials().OAuthToken()
StoreInDatabase(oauthToken)

Steps.Title: Storing an access token for reuse

Nodes.Text: To authorize a client using a stored access token, just set the access token in Configuration along with the other configuration parameters before creating the client:

Steps.Title: Creating a client from a stored token

Nodes.Text: Your application must obtain user authorization before it can execute an endpoint call in case this SDK chooses to use *OAuth 2.0 Implicit Grant* to obtain a user's consent to perform an API request on user's behalf. This authorization includes the following steps
This process requires the presence of a client-side JavaScript code on the redirect URI page to receive the *access token* after the consent step is completed.

To obtain user's consent, you must redirect the user to the authorization page. The `BuildAuthorizationURL` method creates the URL to the authorization page. You must have initialized the client with scopes for which you need permission to access.
Once the user responds to the consent request, the OAuth 2.0 server responds to your application's access request by redirecting the user to the redirect URI specified set in `Configuration`.
The redirect URI will receive the *access token* as the `token` argument in the URL fragment.
https://example.com/oauth/callback#token=XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX
The access token must be extracted by the client-side JavaScript code. The access token can be used to authorize any further endpoint calls by the JavaScript code.

Scopes enable your application to only request access to the resources it needs while enabling users to control the amount of access they grant to your application. Available scopes are defined in the [`OauthScopeFurkotAuthImplicit`]($m/OAuth%20ScopeFurkotAuthImplicit) enumeration.
* [API]($e/API)
The instance of the `Api` class can be accessed from the API Client.
api := client.Api()

Nodes.Title: Trip GET
Nodes.Name: TripGet
Nodes.ResponseText: This method returns an [```ApiResponse```]($h/__additional_documentation/Utilities/ApiResponse) instance. The `Data` property of this instance returns the response data which is of type [[]models.Trip]($m/Trip).

UsageExample.HttpCallTemplate: {"method":"GET","url":"{% case environment %}{% when "production" %}https://trips.furkot.com/pub/api{% else %}envrr{% endcase %}/trip{% capture inline_query_capture_876234 %}{% endcapture %}{% if inline_query_capture_876234.size > 0 %}?{% endif %}{{ inline_query_capture_876234 | remove_first:'&' }}","headers":{% capture header_capture_9023987234 %},"Accept":"application/json"{% if auth["furkot_auth_access_code"] != nil and auth["furkot_auth_access_code"]["OAuthToken"]["access_token"] == nil %},"Authorization":"Bearer {OAUTH_ACCESS_TOKEN}"{% elsif auth["furkot_auth_access_code"]["OAuthToken"]["access_token"] != nil %},"Authorization":{{ auth["furkot_auth_access_code"]["OAuthToken"]["access_token"] | prepend: 'Bearer ' | json: false }}{% endif %}{% endcapture %}{{ '{' }}{{ header_capture_9023987234 | remove_first:',' }}},"body":{{ '{' }}"type":"none"}}

* [Step]($m/Step)
* [Trip]($m/Trip)
* [Coordinates]($m/Coordinates)

Fields.Name: Arrival
Fields.Description: arrival at the stop in its local timezone as YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm

Fields.Name: Departure
Fields.Description: departure from the stop in its local timezone as YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm

Fields.Name: Passthru
Fields.Description: true for pass-through points anchoring route

Fields.Name: Url
Fields.Description: url of the page with more information about the stop

Nodes.Title: Step
Nodes.Name: Step
Nodes.Description: *This model accepts additional fields of type interface{}.*

Fields.Name: Begin
Fields.Description: begin of the trip in its local timezone as YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm

Fields.Name: Description
Fields.Description: description of the trip (truncated to 200 characters)

Fields.Name: End
Fields.Description: end of the trip in its local timezone as YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm

Nodes.Title: Trip
Nodes.Name: Trip
Nodes.Description: *This model accepts additional fields of type interface{}.*

Nodes.Title: Coordinates
Nodes.Name: Coordinates
Nodes.Description:
geographical coordinates of the stop

*This model accepts additional fields of type interface{}.*

Fields.Name: Polyline
Fields.Description: route path compatible with Google polyline encoding algorithm

Nodes.Title: Route
Nodes.Name: Route
Nodes.Description:
route leading to the stop

*This model accepts additional fields of type interface{}.*

Fields.Name: ExpiresIn
Fields.Description: Time in seconds before the access token expires

Fields.Name: Scope
Fields.Description:
List of scopes granted
This is a space-delimited list of strings.

Fields.Name: Expiry
Fields.Description: Time of token expiry as unix timestamp (UTC)

Fields.Name: RefreshToken
Fields.Description:
Refresh token
Used to get a new access token when it expires.

Client authentication failed (e.g., unknown client, no client authentication included, or unsupported authentication method).

The provided authorization grant (e.g., authorization code, resource owner credentials) or refresh token is invalid, expired, revoked, does not match the redirection URI used in the authorization request, or was issued to another client.
The authenticated client is not authorized to use this authorization grant type.
The authorization grant type is not supported by the authorization server.
The requested scope is invalid, unknown, malformed, or exceeds the scope granted by the resource owner.

Gets or sets human-readable text providing additional information on error.
Used to assist the client developer in understanding the error that occurred.

Fields.Description: Gets or sets a URI identifying a human-readable web page with information about the error, used to provide the client developer with additional information about the error.

Represents the logging configurations for API calls.
Methods

Data: Takes in your custom implementation of the LoggerInterface. **Default: `ConsoleLogger`**
Defines the log message severity (e.g., DEBUG, INFO, etc). **Default : `Level_INFO`**
Toggles the global setting to mask sensitive HTTP headers in both requests and responses before logging, safeguarding confidential data. **Default : `true`**
The logging configurations for an API request.
The logging configurations for an API response.

Nodes.Text: Usage Example
Client Initialization with Custom Logger

In order to provide custom logger, any implementation of the `LoggerInterface` can be used so that you can override the `Log` behavior and provide its instance directly in the SDK client initialization.
The following example uses [`Logrus`](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/sirupsen/logrus) implementation of `LoggerInterface` for FurkotTripsClient initialization.
Represents the logging configurations for API requests.
Methods

Data: Toggles the inclusion of query parameters in the logged request path. **Default : `false`**
Toggles the logging of the request body. **Default : `false`**
Toggles the logging of the request headers. **Default : `false`**
Includes only specified request headers (provided as variadic string arguments) in the log output. **Default : `[]string{}`**
Excludes specified request headers (provided as variadic string arguments) from the log output. **Default : `[]string{}`**
Logs specified request headers (provided as variadic string arguments) without masking, revealing their actual values. **Default : `[]string{}`**

Nodes.Text: Represents the logging configurations for API responses.
Methods

Data: Toggles the logging of the request body. **Default : `false`**
Toggles the logging of the request headers. **Default : `false`**
Includes only specified request headers (provided as variadic string arguments) in the log output. **Default : `[]string{}`**
Excludes specified request headers (provided as variadic string arguments) from the log output. **Default : `[]string{}`**
Logs specified request headers (provided as variadic string arguments) without masking, revealing their actual values. **Default : `[]string{}`**

Nodes.Text: The following parameters are configurable for the RetryConfiguration:
Properties

Data:
Whether to retry on request timeout.
*Default*: `true`

Interval before next retry. Used in calculation of wait time for next request in case of failure.
*Default*: `1`

Overall wait time for the requests getting retried.
*Default*: `0`

Used in calculation of wait time for next request in case of failure.
*Default*: `2`

Nodes.Text: The retryConfiguration can be initialized as follows:

```go
package main

import (
    "furkotTrips"
)

func main() {
    retryConfiguration := furkotTrips.CreateRetryConfiguration(
        furkotTrips.WithMaxRetryAttempts(0),
        furkotTrips.WithRetryOnTimeout(true),
        furkotTrips.WithRetryInterval(1),
        furkotTrips.WithMaximumRetryWaitTime(0),
        furkotTrips.WithBackoffFactor(2),
        furkotTrips.WithHttpStatusCodesToRetry([]int64{408, 413, 429, 500, 502, 503, 504, 521, 522, 524, 408, 413, 429, 500, 502, 503, 504, 521, 522, 524}),
        furkotTrips.WithHttpMethodsToRetry([]string{"GET", "PUT", "GET", "PUT"}),
    )
}
```
Represents the result of an API call, including response metadata and the returned data of type `T`.

CodeExamples.Title: Usage Example

Nodes.Text: This developer portal is powered by **APIMatic**. The same platform that generated this experience can turn any OpenAPI specification into a complete developer portal — interactive documentation, auto-generated SDKs in multiple languages, live API testing, and AI-assisted onboarding — in minutes.

Context Plugins load your full API context — docs, SDKs, code samples, and setup instructions — into AI coding environments like Claude Code and Cursor, so developers integrate your API through natural language instead of a wall of reference docs. A built-in portal copilot is available too.

API Reference documentation with language‑specific docs and SDK guides — including idiomatic, ready-to-run code samples complete with imports across all seven SDK languages. Developers can explore your API structure before writing any code.
Built-in testing interface supporting all major authentication methods. Developers can make real API calls, see actual responses, and validate their integration approach.
Production-ready client libraries in Java, Python, TypeScript, C#, PHP, Ruby, and Go. Each SDK includes comprehensive documentation, code samples, and follows language-specific best practices.
Step-by-step onboarding flows to help developers implement key API usecases faster.
Full control over colors, fonts, logos, and layout. Add custom documentation pages, modify navigation, and ensure the portal reflects your brand identity.
Bring your API to APIMatic and ship a developer portal like this one in minutes.
See how APIMatic turns your API specification into a branded, AI-ready developer portal.
Dive into the Advanced Features Guide to set up and customize your own portal.

Nodes.Title: How quickly can we launch our Developer Portal?

Nodes.Text: You can generate and publish your Developer Portal in minutes. Simply upload your OpenAPI specification, customize the look and feel to match your brand, and publish instantly - either as a standalone site or embedded into your existing Developer Portal.

Nodes.Title: How does the Portal stay current as our API evolves?

Nodes.Text: APIMatic automatically updates your portal when your API specification changes. You can re-import updated API definitions, and the portal will regenerate with the latest endpoints, parameters, and documentation while preserving your customizations.

Nodes.Title: What level of customization and control do we have?

Nodes.Text: You can completely customize the look, feel and behavior of your Developer Experience Portal, including primary colors, themes, custom sections, navigation links, custom URL slugs, and branding. You can also choose to host it on your own custom domain or embed it seamlessly into your existing documentation site using a simple HTML script.

Nodes.Title: Is there a way we can integrate this with our existing Developer Portal?

Nodes.Text: Absolutely. APIMatic provides embeddable portal components that can be seamlessly integrated into your existing documentation sites using a simple HTML script. You can also choose to host the portal separately on your own domain while maintaining your existing portal structure.

Nodes.Title: What AI capabilities does this Developer Portal have?

Nodes.Text: **Context Plugins** let developers connect your API to the AI coding assistant they already use (Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Claude, and more) to search, ask questions, and generate working integration code grounded in your live specification.

The portal also includes a built-in **API Copilot** that answers questions from your API reference and product documentation, and an **AI Assist Menu** that sends any documentation page straight to your preferred AI tool as context with one click.
Automatic **LLMs.txt generation** and per-page Markdown exports make your documentation AI-accessible to tools like Cursor, GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Claude.
